teachingPoint: A three-layer security gate on query execution must be applied in a fixed, immutable order: quota check (layer 1) → row-level security (layer 2) → PII masking (layer 3) → execute. Reordering any layer creates exploitable windows. Executing before PII masking exposes raw personal data in query results. Skipping quota check before RLS wastes expensive RLS evaluation on quota-exceeded sessions. The gate order is a MACHINE constraint — not a business preference.
positiveExample: T173 QueryExecutionEngine applies gates in immutable order: T187.check() at layer 1, F422.apply() at layer 2, F423.mask() at layer 3, then executes. Any gate failure emits QueryFailed with correct reason and returns immediately.
negativeExample: T173 injects T187 via @Inject() as F426, or reorders gates to execute before F423 PII masking, or allows tenants to disable F422 RLS. Any of these results in a BUILD_FAILURE.
discriminatingConstraint: Gate order T187(1)→F422(2)→F423(3) is immutable. Reordering or injecting T187 via factory = score-0.
keywords: security gate order quota RLS PII masking immutable query execution layer T187 F422 F423

teachingPoint: T187 QuotaManager is an inline service — it has no factory ID and F426 does not exist. Injecting T187 via @Inject() would make the quota check bypass-able through factory configuration, violating the platform-only enforcement contract. T187 must be instantiated with new QuotaManager(...) directly in the T173 constructor so it cannot be mocked, swapped, or disabled by tenant configuration.
positiveExample: T173 constructor contains: this.quotaManager = new QuotaManager(this.db, this.queue, tenantId). No @Inject, no F426 reference anywhere in the codebase.
negativeExample: T173 declares @Inject('F426') private readonly quotaManager: IQuotaManagerService in its constructor. F426 does not exist — this is a fabricated factory reference that bypasses the inline enforcement requirement.
discriminatingConstraint: T187 instantiated inline in T173 constructor — any @Inject or F426 reference = score-0.
keywords: inline service T187 QuotaManager factory injection F426 does not exist constructor instantiation

teachingPoint: Data retention enforcement must follow a mandatory four-step execution order: legal hold check → approval token validation → governance decision → purge. Skipping the legal hold check can purge legally protected content. Purging without an approval token removes the audit chain required for compliance. Reordering governance before approval allows governance to approve a purge that would later be blocked by a missing token, causing a partial state problem. The DataPurged event must contain only tombstoneRef — no raw content fields.
positiveExample: T186 DataRetentionEnforcer: (1) ILegalHoldService.check() → ContentRetentionExtended on hold, (2) IApprovalService.validate() → reject on missing/expired token, (3) IDataGovernanceService.enforceRetention() → reject on governance block, (4) emit DataPurged with tombstoneRef only.
negativeExample: T186 skips legal hold check and goes directly to approval. A legally held document is purged. Or T186 emits DataPurged with a rawContent field — compliance audit fails because the event proves data was transmitted after purge.
discriminatingConstraint: Legal hold at ORDER 1, tombstoneRef-only in DataPurged — any other order or rawContent field = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: data retention execution order legal hold approval governance purge tombstoneRef DataPurged compliance

teachingPoint: All warehouse query operations — including cross-flow correlation and multi-join analytics — must include tenantId in every predicate. Omitting tenantId from a single join in a multi-step cross-flow query allows one tenant's data to appear in another tenant's analytics results. This is not detectable at the gateway level because each individual query step may pass tenant validation while the cross-join step silently mixes rows. The only safe rule is: every searchDocuments call includes tenantId.
positiveExample: T180 CrossFlowCorrelationEngine fetches each source flow with: { tenantId, sourceFlowId: flowId }. T176 CohortAnalysisEngine always scopes cohort membership with { tenantId, cohortId }.
negativeExample: T180 joins flow results without tenantId predicate on the cross-flow index. Tenant A's correlation result includes Tenant B's event counts.
discriminatingConstraint: tenantId in every searchDocuments predicate — any cross-tenant join = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: tenant isolation cross flow join predicate tenantId scope analytics correlation multi-tenant

teachingPoint: Export operations require three independent platform-level protections applied in order: PII masking → encryption → audit logging. Each protection layer is PLATFORM-ONLY — tenants cannot disable any of them. PII masking must run first so that encrypted output never contains unmasked personal data. Audit logging must record every export including failed ones. A downloadUrl in the DataExportReady event must carry an expiresAt field — permanent download links violate data minimization requirements.
positiveExample: T181 DataExportEngine: (1) F423 PII masking → (2) F411 encryption → (3) storeDocument → (4) F412 audit log → (5) emit DataExportReady with downloadUrl and expiresAt. No bypass, no skipMasking flag.
negativeExample: T181 allows tenants to pass skipPiiMasking=true for internal datasets. Encrypted export still contains unmasked employee records — compliance violation. Or T181 emits DataExportReady without expiresAt — permanent link remains accessible after retention period.
discriminatingConstraint: F423 before F411 before F412, downloadUrl requires expiresAt — any bypass or missing expiresAt = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: data export PII masking encryption audit expiry download URL platform only F411 F412 F423
three layer security gate quota RLS PII masking order immutable T187 F422 F423 query execution
teachingPoint: T173 QueryExecutionEngine three-layer security gate order is MANDATORY AND IMMUTABLE: T187 quota (INLINE, layer 1) → F422 RLS (layer 2) → F423 PII masking (layer 3) → executeQuery. Any reordering is a BUILD_FAILURE.
ironRules: LAYER 1: T187 QuotaManager.check() — INLINE, not factory-injected, runs first
LAYER 2: F422 IRowLevelSecurityService.apply() — PLATFORM-ONLY, cannot be disabled
LAYER 3: F423 IPIIMaskingService.mask() — PLATFORM-ONLY, runs BEFORE result serialization
Any gate failure: emit QueryFailed with correct reason, return immediately
recordUsage() called AFTER successful execution — never before

keywords: inline service T187 QuotaManager factory injection F426 does not exist constructor instantiation platform
teachingPoint: T187 QuotaManager is an INLINE service — F426 does NOT exist. T187 is instantiated directly in the T173 constructor via new QuotaManager(config) and must NEVER be @Inject()-ed or resolved via any factory.
ironRules: T187 has no factory ID — F426 does NOT exist in the system
T187 is instantiated with: this.quotaManager = new QuotaManager(this.db, this.queue, tenantId)
No @Inject('F426') or any factory token for T187
quota.check() runs at layer 1 before any warehouse access
quota.recordUsage() runs only after successful query completion

keywords: data retention execution order legal hold approval token governance purge DataPurged tombstoneRef irreversible
teachingPoint: T186 DataRetentionEnforcer execution order is MANDATORY AND INVIOLABLE: (1) legal hold check → (2) approval token → (3) governance → (4) purge+emit DataPurged with tombstoneRef ONLY.
ironRules: ORDER 1: ILegalHoldService.check() — holdStatus.active → emit ContentRetentionExtended, return
ORDER 2: IApprovalService.validate() — missing/expired token → reject without purge, return
ORDER 3: IDataGovernanceService (F424) enforceRetention() — get purge decision
ORDER 4: Emit DataPurged with tombstoneRef ONLY — PROHIBITED: rawContent, content, payload, body, data
F424 IDataGovernanceService is PLATFORM-ONLY — not tenant-editable
